summary: Iteration 13 — verification of the booking-confirmation ROOT-CAUSE fix (web3forms bypass removal + backend rewiring + warmer email/UI copy). ALL 13 pytest assertions passed + full Playwright E2E on live preview URL passed on both booking and contact flows. Contact.jsx source is clean of 'web3forms' and 'access_key' (zero occurrences), both forms POST to ${REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL}/api/{bookings,contact}. Browser E2E on /contact: (a) BookingForm — filled name/email/date(+7d)/time=11:00 → toast 'Booking received. Confirmation on its way.' visible + booking-success div renders 'Booked. Confirmation and calendar invite for 2026-07-22 at 11:00 (UTC) coming from hello@globisync.com.'; backend logs (15:02:16) show TWO 'Email sent to=' lines within 100ms — globisyncltd@gmail.com (resend_id b871a164-fa0d-46a2-b759-014a0237dd95, subject 'New discovery-call booking from E2E Test — GlobiSync') AND e2e-booking-test@example.com (resend_id 752f20b0-68d2-4890-8d0d-8d8e2fa2963b, subject 'Your discovery-call request — GlobiSync' — em dash ✓); Mongo bookings row persisted with matching name/email/date/time. (b) ContactForm — filled name/email/message → toast 'Received. We'll reply within one working day.' visible + contact-success div 'Thank you. A senior expert will reply within one working day.'; backend logs show TWO 'Email sent to=' lines within 20ms — admin + submitter (submitter subject 'Thanks — we've received your message — GlobiSync' — NOW ALSO USES EM DASH, resolving the iteration-11 low-priority middle-dot note); Mongo contact_submissions row persisted. server.py _booking_confirmation_html contains 'Simply reply to this email', 'Lovely to hear from you', 'One of our senior team will personally reach out' AND does NOT contain 'A senior expert will send you a calendar invite' or 'Email us at hello@globisync.com'. _thank_you_html contains 'Lovely to hear from you', 'One of our senior team will personally reply', 'Feel free to reply to this email directly' AND does NOT contain 'A senior expert from our Birmingham desk'. Contact page Response promise renders the new long copy 'A real human — not a bot — will read your message and reply within one working day. In writing, in your inbox, from someone who actually runs accounts every day.' (verified visually + in source). Backend GET /api/sitemap.xml → 200, 103 <url> entries. Static /app/frontend/public/{_redirects (SPA fallback last, legacy 301s present), vercel.json, sitemap.xml (103), robots.txt (Disallow /api/ + /static/)} all intact.
critical_code_review_comments: Booking success-div copy references 'calendar invite ... coming from hello@globisync.com' but the corresponding email HTML now explicitly says 'One of our senior team will personally reach out ... to confirm the slot and drop a calendar invite in your inbox' — the two messages are consistent, but if E1 wants to further simplify, the UI copy could say 'Confirmation coming from hello@globisync.com within one working day' to avoid promising the calendar invite lands automatically at submission time. Non-blocking.

Contact success-div still shows the older short copy 'A senior expert will reply within one working day.' — the warmer language ('Lovely to hear from you', 'One of our senior team will personally reply', 'reply to this email directly') was added ONLY to the email body, not to this on-page confirmation. Aligning the on-page confirmation copy with the email tone would improve brand voice consistency. Non-blocking.

server.py is 541 lines and CONTACT.jsx is 300+ lines — E1 could split email templates into /app/backend/email_templates.py and extract ContactForm / BookingForm into /app/frontend/src/components/contact/{ContactForm.jsx,BookingForm.jsx} for cleaner separation. Carried over from iteration 11/12.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (both E2E flows — booking + contact — success div + toast + backend emails + mongo persistence)

test_credentials: N/A — no auth on any tested endpoint.
seed_data_creation: Two live rows created on this preview environment: (1) bookings collection — id=24e0b0b8-b38f-4445-8250-d0422930d993, email=e2e-booking-test@example.com, name='E2E Test', preferred_date='2026-07-22', preferred_time='11:00'. (2) contact_submissions collection — id=d2bb6618-61ca-4a44-b8b4-29fda778a937, email=e2e-contact-test@example.com, name='E2E Contact', message='E2E test message iteration 13'. Plus one extra pytest booking (e2e-booking-<uuid>@example.com) and one pytest contact (e2e-contact-<uuid>@example.com). All use throwaway example.com addresses — submitter Resend sends will bounce as expected. Not cleaned up; safe to leave for future audit.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Root cause of the original bug (web3forms bypassing the backend) is fully fixed. If the same class of bug is reported again, first `grep -R 'web3forms\|access_key' /app/frontend/src` — must return zero hits. Both forms in /app/frontend/src/pages/Contact.jsx now use axios.post(`${API}/{contact,bookings}`) where API = ${process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL}/api. Regression suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_13_e2e.py — re-run with `cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/test_iteration_13_e2e.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/iteration_13.xml`. Existing tests (test_email_reply_to.py, test_careers.py, test_iteration_9_smoke.py, backend_test.py) still valid and orthogonal. When retesting with real inbox, replace e2e-*@example.com with a real address to confirm actual delivery + reply-to header land on hello@globisync.com (env var REPLY_TO_EMAIL in /app/backend/.env is already set).
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE (fixed by E1 in this iteration): Both ContactForm and BookingForm in /app/frontend/src/pages/Contact.jsx were POSTing directly to https://api.web3forms.com/submit with a hardcoded access_key `01b3e79b-48a6-416d-a8eb-7b63582d697c`. This third-party form service delivered the admin notification to globisyncltd@gmail.com (so admins saw them, which explains why the bug wasn't caught earlier) BUT never triggered the FastAPI backend's /api/bookings or /api/contact endpoints, meaning the Resend-based two-email flow (admin + submitter confirmation with reply_to=hello@globisync.com) was completely bypassed. This is why submitters reported not receiving confirmations. FIX APPLIED: (1) Removed all web3forms endpoints + access_key from Contact.jsx and rewired both handlers to axios.post to ${API}/contact and ${API}/bookings, (2) Updated backend _booking_confirmation_html to explicitly instruct 'Simply reply to this email' (leveraging reply_to header set to hello@globisync.com) and 'One of our senior team will personally reach out', (3) Refreshed _thank_you_html with warmer language, (4) Warmed the Contact page 'Response promise' UI block. VERIFIED live end-to-end: browser submission → FastAPI → asyncio.create_task → Resend API → TWO 'Email sent to=' log lines (admin + submitter) with valid Resend UUIDs AND matching Mongo row persistence, all within ~200ms of submission. Also verified as a side-benefit that the contact submitter subject has been normalised from middle dot ('·') to em dash ('—'), resolving the low-priority carry-over from iteration 11.
